title: Methods should be named according to their synchronicities
description: Methods should be named according to their synchronicitiesAnalyze your codeconsistency-identifiablemaintainabilityCode SmellWhy is this an issue?More InfoAccording to the Task-based Asynchronous Pattern (TAP), methods returning either aSystem.Threading.Tasks.Taskor aSystem.Threading.Tasks.Task<TResult>are considered "asynchronous". Such methods should use theAsyncsuffix. Conversely methods which do not return such Tasks should not have an "Async" suffix in their names.Noncompliant code exampleusing System; using System.Threading.Tasks; namespace myLibrary { public class Foo { public Task Read(byte [] buffer, int offset, int count, // Noncompliant CancellationToken cancellationToken) } }Compliant solutionusing System; using System.Threading.Tasks; namespace myLibrary { public class Foo { pu
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.description: Check that methods are named according to their synchronicity (Task returns need Async suffix)
